title: Recent Weakness in Chinese Consumer Spending
section_path: Recent Weakness in Chinese Consumer Spending
section_path_text: Recent Weakness in Chinese Consumer Spending
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
An examination of China's most recently released domestic retail sales data (December's data) shows that domestic appliance sales, furniture sales, and clothing sales all experienced larger year-on-year contractions that had been previously seen in earlier months. This weakness occurred due in part to new coronavirus restrictions that began to be placed in many cities starting in late December. Appliance sales (which includes consumer electronics) in China totaled approximately 93.3 billion yuan in December, which has marked a year-on-year contraction of 8%.
